info.name: CLI Agent Gateway
info.description:
Complete API collection for the CLI Agent Gateway — a WebSocket + HTTP service that wraps Claude Code, Cursor, and Gemini CLI agents behind a unified protocol.

Base URL: http://localhost:8080
WebSocket: ws://localhost:8080/ws

request.description: Liveness probe.
Returns all registered agent adapters with their full capability sets and supported model lists.

response.name: 200 OK

item.name: Authentication
item.description: Per-agent login state management. The gateway checks login before creating a session and caches the result in memory for the server's lifetime.

request.description:
Returns cached (or freshly checked) authentication status for Claude Code.

cacheHit=false → ran `claude auth status` live.
cacheHit=true  → returned from in-memory cache.
Checks Gemini CLI authentication via `gemini auth status`.

response.name: 200 OK
response.body:
{
  "agentName": "gemini",
  "loggedIn": true,
  "detail": "Logged in as user@gmail.com",
  "cacheHit": false,
  "authSupported": true
}

request.description: Cursor has no CLI auth command. Always returns authSupported=false, loggedIn=true.

Starts the Claude Code OAuth login flow. Returns a browser URL immediately. The login process runs in the background — the cache is updated to loggedIn=true automatically when the user completes the OAuth flow.

response.name: 200 — URL Returned

request.description: Starts the Gemini CLI OAuth login flow. Returns a browser URL immediately.

response.name: 200 OK

request.description: Cursor has no CLI auth flow. Returns authSupported=false.

response.name: 200 OK — Auth Not Required

request.description: Clears the cached login state. The next GET /agents/claude-code/auth or session.create will run a fresh live check.

item.name: Models
item.description: Per-agent model listings. Use these IDs in the `model` field of session.create.

request.description: Returns supported model IDs for Claude Code. Short aliases (opus, sonnet, haiku) are accepted by the --model flag.
Returns supported model IDs for Gemini CLI.
Returns supported model IDs for the Cursor CLI agent.
Example 404 for an unregistered agent.
Returns operating modes for Claude Code. Use the mode `name` in the session.create payload.config.

response.name: 200 OK

request.description: Returns operating modes for the Cursor agent.

response.name: 200 OK

request.description: Returns operating modes for the Gemini CLI agent.

response.name: 200 OK

request.description: Returns all registered skills across all scopes.

response.name: 200 OK

request.description: Registers a global skill visible to all agents. Use scope=global or a specific agent name.

response.name: 201 Created

request.description: Registers a skill visible only to claude-code sessions.

response.name: 201 Created

request.description: Removes a skill from the global registry. Sessions that already have it attached are unaffected.
Returns skills visible to claude-code: global + claude-code-scoped.
Returns skills visible to gemini (global only — security-review is claude-code scoped).
Returns all registered MCP server configurations.
Registers a global stdio MCP server available to all agents.
Registers an MCP server scoped to claude-code sessions only.
Registers a remote SSE-based MCP server.
Removes an MCP server from the registry. Sessions that already have it attached are unaffected.
Returns MCP servers visible to claude-code: global + claude-code-scoped.
Returns MCP servers visible to gemini (global only).
Lists all skills available to the session's agent, annotated with their current attachment status.
Attaches a registered skill to a live session. Takes effect on the next prompt. Idempotent.
Detaches a skill from a live session. Takes effect on the next prompt.
Lists all MCP servers available to the session's agent, annotated with their current attachment status.
Attaches a registered MCP server to a live session. Takes effect on the next prompt. Idempotent.
Detaches an MCP server from a live session. Takes effect on the next prompt.

item.name: WebSocket — Message Examples
item.description:
Connect to {{wsUrl}} using Postman's WebSocket client (New → WebSocket Request), then paste the message bodies below.

All messages follow the Envelope schema:
{
  "id": "<uuid>",
  "type": "<message-type>",
  "sessionId": "<session-id>",
  "flow": "<agent-name>",
  "payload": { ... },
  "timestamp": "<ISO-8601>"
}

request.description:
Create a cursor session.

Message body:
```json
{
  "id": "msg-001",
  "type": "session.create",
  "sessionId": "cursor-session-456",
  "flow": "cursor",
  "payload": {
    "connectionMode": "spawn",
    "workingDir": "/tmp/cursor-project"
  },
  "timestamp": "2026-03-21T00:00:00Z"
}
```

Create a gemini session.

Message body:
```json
{
  "id": "msg-001",
  "type": "session.create",
  "sessionId": "gemini-session-789",
  "flow": "gemini",
  "payload": {
    "connectionMode": "spawn",
    "workingDir": "/tmp/gemini-project",
    "model": "gemini-2.5-pro"
  },
  "timestamp": "2026-03-21T00:00:00Z"
}
```

Cancel an in-flight prompt.

Message body:
```json
{
  "id": "msg-004",
  "type": "prompt.cancel",
  "sessionId": "session-abc-123",
  "flow": "claude-code",
  "payload": {},
  "timestamp": "2026-03-21T00:00:03Z"
}
```

Approve a pending tool use (received as tool.use.start server-push).

Message body:
```json
{
  "id": "msg-005",
  "type": "tool.approve",
  "sessionId": "session-abc-123",
  "flow": "claude-code",
  "payload": { "toolId": "toolu_01Abc123" },
  "timestamp": "2026-03-21T00:00:04Z"
}
```

Reject a pending tool use.

Message body:
```json
{
  "id": "msg-006",
  "type": "tool.reject",
  "sessionId": "session-abc-123",
  "flow": "claude-code",
  "payload": {
    "toolId": "toolu_01Abc123",
    "reason": "Do not write to that path"
  },
  "timestamp": "2026-03-21T00:00:04Z"
}
```

Resume an existing session after a WebSocket reconnect. Auto-restarts the agent if the process died.

Message body:
```json
{
  "id": "msg-008",
  "type": "session.resume",
  "sessionId": "session-abc-123",
  "flow": "claude-code",
  "payload": {},
  "timestamp": "2026-03-21T00:00:06Z"
}
```

Response:
```json
{
  "type": "session.resumed",
  "replyTo": "msg-008",
  "payload": {
    "sessionId": "session-abc-123",
    "flow": "claude-code",
    "status": "existing"
  }
}
```

Permanently terminate and remove a session.

Message body:
```json
{
  "id": "msg-009",
  "type": "session.end",
  "sessionId": "session-abc-123",
  "flow": "claude-code",
  "payload": {},
  "timestamp": "2026-03-21T00:00:10Z"
}
```

Response:
```json
{
  "type": "session.ended",
  "replyTo": "msg-009",
  "sessionId": "session-abc-123",
  "payload": null
}
```

item.name: End-to-End Workflows
item.description: Multi-step flows combining HTTP and WebSocket.

item.name: [Workflow Step 1] Check auth before creating session

request.description:
Step 1: Check if the agent is logged in.

If loggedIn=false → call POST /agents/claude-code/auth/login → user visits URL → retry.
If loggedIn=true  → proceed to create session via WebSocket.
Step 2 (only if not logged in): Start the login flow. Copy the returned URL and open it in a browser. Wait for the OAuth flow to complete, then retry Step 1 to confirm loggedIn=true.
Step 3: Fetch the model list to pick a model ID for the session.

Step 4: Register a skill globally.

Next steps:
- Create session via WebSocket: session.create with model from Step 3
- Attach skill: POST /sessions/{id}/skills/go-expert
- Send prompt: WebSocket prompt.send
- The skill is injected automatically as --append-system-prompt
